_comment: Phase 3 analysis table schema. The analysis columns mirror the ground-truth dataset Test_Datasets/test_dataset_benchmarking_analysis.csv (';'-delimited, 28 columns). The simplified Test_Datasets/test_dataset_benchmarking_numbers.csv is a 6-column subset (DTx Name, Is it a DiGA?, Company \ Provider, Evidence Type, Trial Registration ID, Sources / Publications) used to benchmark Phase 2 study counts; the converter fills those 6 keys and leaves the rest empty. 'key' is the JSON field used in Phase 3 output. 'label' is the EXACT CSV header (matched after stripping surrounding whitespace). 'fill' = where the value comes from: 'dtx' (from data/dtx_data*.json), 'study' (from the verified studies.json object), or 'study_or_llm' (prefer the scraped study value, otherwise let the LLM extract it from the raw evidence file). 'study_keys' lists the keys to try inside a study object. 'description' guides the LLM when it must fill a gap.

columns.label: Category
columns.description: Short (1-3 word) medical/therapeutic category of the DTx. When the dataset 'category'/'dtx_category' field is empty, it is LLM-derived from the DTx name, description and clinical area (ICD-10).

columns.label: Evidence Type
columns.description: Type of evidence / study design (e.g. RCT, RWE, Pilot RCT, cohort study).

columns.label: Primary End Point Duration in weeks
columns.description: Duration until the primary endpoint is assessed, expressed in weeks (number only when possible).

columns.label: Follow-up after Primary End Point
columns.description: Whether and when participants were followed up after the primary endpoint (e.g. '6 months', 'yes').

columns.label: Duration Additional Info
columns.description: Any additional notes about the study or endpoint duration/timing.

columns.label: Study Size (participants)
columns.description: Total number of participants enrolled or analyzed in the study.

columns.label: Intervention and Control group
columns.description: Description and/or sizes of the intervention and control groups (e.g. 'I = 164 and C = 173').

columns.label: Additional Info About the Trial Arms
columns.description: Details describing what each trial arm received (intervention vs control specifics).

columns.label: Collected data (e.g. PROMS, PHQ-9, pain level etc.)
columns.description: The instruments, measures, scales, questionnaires and variables COLLECTED/tracked during the study (NOT the results), e.g. PROMs, PHQ-9, IBS-SSS, NPRS pain level, adherence logs. Primary Outcome and Secondary Outcome MEASURE definitions (what is measured and when) belong here.

columns.label: Key Outcomes / Findings
columns.description: ONLY the actual REPORTED results/findings of the study: effect sizes, percentage changes, p-values, statistical significance, responder rates and conclusions. Return "" when the source only DEFINES what will be measured (endpoints / outcome-measure definitions, e.g. registry or ClinicalTrials.gov protocols) and reports no results - those measures belong in collected_data, not here. Do not invent results.

columns.label: Trial Registration ID
columns.description: Trial registration / study identifier. Prefer the registry id (NCT########, DRKS########, ISRCTN########); for PubMed use the PMID. For website sources, look for any registration id (NCT/DRKS/ISRCTN/EUCTR) mentioned in the raw text.

columns.label: Trial Start Date
columns.description: Date the trial started, formatted as 'DD Month YYYY' (e.g. '04 March 2018'); if the day is unknown use 'Month YYYY'; if only the year is known use 'YYYY'.

columns.label: Trial End Date
columns.description: Date the trial ended or was completed, formatted as 'DD Month YYYY' (e.g. '04 March 2018'); if the day is unknown use 'Month YYYY'; if only the year is known use 'YYYY'.

columns.label: publication date
columns.description: Publication date of the study/article, formatted as 'DD Month YYYY' (e.g. '04 March 2018'); if the day is unknown use 'Month YYYY'; if only the year is known use 'YYYY'.

columns.label: Sources / Publications
columns.description: URL(s) or citation(s) for the study/publication.
